field_label_if_available: The higher of a SNF's achievement score and improvement score; scores range from 0 to 100. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, SNFs were assigned a performance score of 0.00000 to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF was assigned a net-neutral performance score of 5.15328 as calculated by CMS to receive a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier.

field_label_if_available: This multiplier represents the SNF VBP payment incentive after taking into account the 2% withhold that funds the Program. When making payments to SNF claims, the adjusted federal per diem rate will be multiplied by this factor. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, each SNF received an identical incentive payment multiplier of 0.992 to reflect a net payment reduction of 0.8%, as discussed in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF received a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier of 1.

field_name: Performance Period: 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 National Average Readmission Rate
field_label_if_available:
The SNF VBP Program national unadjusted unplanned readmission rate for 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 at the stay level.

Note: In response to the COVID-19 public health emergency, CMS excluded qualifying claims in the first two quarters of Calendar Year (CY) 2020 (January 1, 2020 – June 30, 2020) from use in the SNF VBP Program. Additionally, because the SNFRM assesses hospital readmissions within 30 days of discharge from a prior hospitalization to a SNF and requires data from 12 months prior to the start of the SNF stay for risk adjustment, residents who were discharged from the hospital to the SNF after December 1, 2019 and residents with SNF admissions from July 1, 2020 – September 30, 2020 were excluded from the SNFRM calculations due to missing data. Thus, the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program performance period originally specified in the interim final rule with comment period was restricted to April 1, 2019 through December 1, 2019. For further information please see pages 54835 through 54837 of the interim final rule with comment period (85 FR 54820) and the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule (86 FR 42424).

field_label_if_available: The higher of a SNF's achievement score and improvement score; scores range from 0 to 100. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, SNFs were assigned a performance score of 0.00000 to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF was assigned a net-neutral performance score of 5.15328 as calculated by CMS to receive a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier.

field_label_if_available: This multiplier represents the SNF VBP payment incentive after taking into account the 2% withhold that funds the Program. When making payments to SNF claims, the adjusted federal per diem rate will be multiplied by this factor. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, each SNF received an identical incentive payment multiplier of 0.992 to reflect a net payment reduction of 0.8%, as discussed in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF received a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier of 1.

field_name: Performance Period: 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 National Average Readmission Rate
field_label_if_available:
The SNF VBP Program national unadjusted unplanned readmission rate for 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 at the stay level.

Note: In response to the COVID-19 public health emergency, CMS excluded qualifying claims in the first two quarters of Calendar Year (CY) 2020 (January 1, 2020 – June 30, 2020) from use in the SNF VBP Program. Additionally, because the SNFRM assesses hospital readmissions within 30 days of discharge from a prior hospitalization to a SNF and requires data from 12 months prior to the start of the SNF stay for risk adjustment, residents who were discharged from the hospital to the SNF after December 1, 2019 and residents with SNF admissions from July 1, 2020 – September 30, 2020 were excluded from the SNFRM calculations due to missing data. Thus, the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program performance period originally specified in the interim final rule with comment period was restricted to April 1, 2019 through December 1, 2019. For further information please see pages 54835 through 54837 of the interim final rule with comment period (85 FR 54820) and the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule (86 FR 42424).

field_label_if_available: The higher of a SNF's achievement score and improvement score; scores range from 0 to 100. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, SNFs were assigned a performance score of 0.00000 to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF was assigned a net-neutral performance score of 5.15328 as calculated by CMS to receive a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier.
notes: Performance Score | The higher of a SNF's achievement score and improvement score; scores range from 0 to 100. field_label_if_available: This multiplier represents the SNF VBP payment incentive after taking into account the 2% withhold that funds the Program. When making payments to SNF claims, the adjusted federal per diem rate will be multiplied by this factor. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, each SNF received an identical incentive payment multiplier of 0.992 to reflect a net payment reduction of 0.8%, as discussed in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF received a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier of 1.

field_name: Performance Period: 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 National Average Readmission Rate
field_label_if_available:
The SNF VBP Program national unadjusted unplanned readmission rate for 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 at the stay level.

Note: In response to the COVID-19 public health emergency, CMS excluded qualifying claims in the first two quarters of Calendar Year (CY) 2020 (January 1, 2020 – June 30, 2020) from use in the SNF VBP Program. Additionally, because the SNFRM assesses hospital readmissions within 30 days of discharge from a prior hospitalization to a SNF and requires data from 12 months prior to the start of the SNF stay for risk adjustment, residents who were discharged from the hospital to the SNF after December 1, 2019 and residents with SNF admissions from July 1, 2020 – September 30, 2020 were excluded from the SNFRM calculations due to missing data. Thus, the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program performance period originally specified in the interim final rule with comment period was restricted to April 1, 2019 through December 1, 2019. For further information please see pages 54835 through 54837 of the interim final rule with comment period (85 FR 54820) and the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule (86 FR 42424).

field_label_if_available: The higher of a SNF's achievement score and improvement score; scores range from 0 to 100. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, SNFs were assigned a performance score of 0.00000 to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF was assigned a net-neutral performance score of 5.15328 as calculated by CMS to receive a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier.

field_label_if_available: This multiplier represents the SNF VBP payment incentive after taking into account the 2% withhold that funds the Program. When making payments to SNF claims, the adjusted federal per diem rate will be multiplied by this factor. In the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, CMS suppressed the use of SNF readmission measure data for purposes of FY 2022 scoring and payment adjustments in the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program year because the effects of the COVID-19 public health emergency on the data used to calculate the SNFRM inhibited CMS's ability to make fair national comparisons of SNFs' performance. Per this policy, each SNF received an identical incentive payment multiplier of 0.992 to reflect a net payment reduction of 0.8%, as discussed in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule, to mitigate the effect that the COVID-19 public health emergency would otherwise have had on SNFs' performance scores and incentive payment multipliers. CMS then applied the low-volume adjustment policy as previously finalized in the FY 2019 SNF PPS final rule and discussed maintaining the LVA policy for the FY 2022 Program year in the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule. If a SNF had fewer than 25 eligible stays during the performance period (April 1, 2019, through December 1, 2019), the SNF received a net-neutral incentive payment multiplier of 1.

field_name: Performance Period: 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 National Average Readmission Rate
field_label_if_available:
The SNF VBP Program national unadjusted unplanned readmission rate for 4/1/2019-12/1/2019 at the stay level.

Note: In response to the COVID-19 public health emergency, CMS excluded qualifying claims in the first two quarters of Calendar Year (CY) 2020 (January 1, 2020 – June 30, 2020) from use in the SNF VBP Program. Additionally, because the SNFRM assesses hospital readmissions within 30 days of discharge from a prior hospitalization to a SNF and requires data from 12 months prior to the start of the SNF stay for risk adjustment, residents who were discharged from the hospital to the SNF after December 1, 2019 and residents with SNF admissions from July 1, 2020 – September 30, 2020 were excluded from the SNFRM calculations due to missing data. Thus, the FY 2022 SNF VBP Program performance period originally specified in the interim final rule with comment period was restricted to April 1, 2019 through December 1, 2019. For further information please see pages 54835 through 54837 of the interim final rule with comment period (85 FR 54820) and the FY 2022 SNF PPS final rule (86 FR 42424).
